converted from EAD 1.0 to EAD 2002 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Gordon and Brysis Whitnall papers
Whitnall, Gordon, 1888-1977.
2880
22.6 cubic feet. 22.6 cubic feet. 12 tape recordings, 9 transcripts.,
1913-1975. Ordinances, maps, reports, correspondence, course outlines, interview tapes and transcript, publications, family and biographical material, income tax returns; and other miscellaneous business and personal papers. Contains material on Gordon and Brysis Whitnall's careers as planners as well as family and biographical records. Collection material in English <<<<<<< HEAD 24 23 39 - trans. 2880 ======= 24 23 39 - trans. 2880 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab 2068810
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
M. Warren
Date completed:
July 1979
EAD encoding:
Martin Heggestad, Ann Hubert, May - July 2002
Date modified:
Marcie Farwell, October 2015
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
In 1913, Gordon Whitnall founded the Los Angeles City Planning Association, and in 1920, he established the Los Angeles City Planning Department. From 1920-1930, he was Director of Planning for Los Angeles, and from 1929-1930 was president of the League of California Cities. From 1932-1935 he was the coordinator of the Committee on Government Simplification for Los Angeles County. In 1941, Gordon and Brysis Whitnall established a planning and government consulting firm in Los Angeles. Gordon Whitnall was an instructor in Planning at the University of Southern California, and a member of the American Society of Planning Officials, the American Institute of Planners, the American Society of Consulting Planners, and the International Fraternity of Lambda Alpha, Los Angeles Chapter.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Includes ordinances, maps, reports, and correspondence for cities in the West and Southwest, including Amarillo, Texas; Albuquerque, New Mexico; Seattle, Washington; and Los Angeles, Modesto, Glendale, Palm Springs, and numerous other cities in California. Personal files consist of course outlines; Gordon and Brysis Whitnall interview tapes and transcript; material on Charles B. Whitnall Park; professional society correspondence, publications, and research information concerning the American Institute of Planners, the American Society of Planning Officials, and other organizations; published articles and reports; and tape recording of a speech at Cornell University, "After a Half Century of Urban Zoning - What's the Verdict." Also, family and biographical material; income tax returns; and other miscellaneous business and personal papers.
SERIES LIST
Series I. Personal Files Box 11 and 15
Series II. Ordinances, Maps, Reports, and Correspondence (Alpha and Date Files) Boxes 1-11; 15-22
Alphabetical File I Alphabetical File II Alphabetical File III Files Arranged By Date
Series III. Professional Societies: Correspondence, Publications, Research Information Box 12-13
Series IV. Papers by Gordon Whitnall (a few by others) Box 14
Cite As:
Gordon and Brysis Whitnall Papers, #2880. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Lambda Alpha International. -- Los Angeles Chapter. Speeches. Maps. Interviews. Seattle (Wash.) -- City planning. Palm Springs (Calif.) -- City planning. Modesto (Calif.) -- City planning. Los Angeles County (Calif.) Los Angeles (Calif.) -- City planning. Glendale (Calif.) -- City planning. Amarillo (Tex.) -- City planning. Albuquerque (N.M.) -- City planning. City planners. Zoning. City planning. Whitnall, Brysis. American Institute of Planners American Society of Consulting Planners American Society of Planning Officials League of California Cities Los Angeles (Calif.). City Planning Association Los Angeles (Calif.). City Planning Department Los Angeles County (Calif.). Committee on Government Simplification University of Southern California. School of Architecture University of Southern California. School of Public Administration
